{"schema_version":"0.4.0","event":{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"call_5Co7RSKpyby7YJaZyYnH9UBU:a99d795f:output","type":"tool_output","call_id":"call_5Co7RSKpyby7YJaZyYnH9UBU:a99d795f","tool_call_id":"call_5Co7RSKpyby7YJaZyYnH9UBU","output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to VT Code coding agent, when working with code in this repository. Please make sure the application is \"VT Code\", with proper capitalization and spacing.\n\nThis file is the **entry-point map** for agents working on VT Code. Deep knowledge lives in `docs/` — this file tells you WHERE to look, not WHAT to do.\n\n## Personality & Communication\n\nDefault personality and tone when working on VT Code:\n\n- **Concise and direct**: Minimize output; answer specifically without elaboration or flattery\n- **Actionable**: Always prioritize next steps, assumptions, and clear guidance\n- **Efficient**: Avoid verbose explanations unless explicitly requested\n- **Collaborative**: Work like a knowledgeable teammate; share momentum and progress\n\nExample: Instead of \"That's a great question about the architecture,\" jump directly to the analysis or answer.\n\n## Workspace Structure\n\nVT Code uses a **13-member Cargo workspace**:\n\n```\nvtcode/                          # Binary entrypoint (src/main.rs)\n├── vtcode-core/                 # Core library (LLM, tools, config, MCP)\n├── vtcode-tui/                  # TUI surface and session runtime\n├── vtcode-config/               # Configuration loader & schema\n├── vtcode-commons/              # Shared utilities\n├── vtcode-llm/                  # LLM provider abstractions\n├── vtcode-lmstudio/             # LM Studio provider integration\n├── vtcode-tools/                # Tool implementations\n├── vtcode-bash-runner/          # Shell execution engine\n├── vtcode-markdown-store/       # Document storage\n├── vtcode-indexer/              # Code indexing\n├── vtcode-exec-events/          # Event definitions\n├── vtcode-file-search/          # Parallel fuzzy file search\n└── vtcode-acp-client/           # Agent Client Protocol bridge\n```\n\n`vtcode-process-hardening` is intentionally excluded from `[workspace].members` and remains an isolated pre-main hardening crate.\n\n**Key separation**:\n\n- **vtcode-core/**: Reusable library with 77% complexity reduction through mode-based execution\n- **src/**: CLI executable (Ratatui TUI, PTY, slash commands)\n\n## Architecture Highlights\n\n### Core Systems\n\n**LLM** (`vtcode-llm/`, `vtcode-core/src/llm/`): Factory pattern, 10 providers, failover, caching, token budgets.\n\n**Tools** (`vtcode-tools/`, `vtcode-core/src/tools/`): Trait-driven (`Tool`, `ModeTool`, `CacheableTool`). Unified: `unified_exec`, `unified_file`, `unified_search`.\n\n**Config**: Env vars → `vtcode.toml` → constants. Never hardcode.\n\n**PTY** (`vtcode-core/src/exec/`, `vtcode-bash-runner/`): Interactive shell sessions, streaming output.\n\n**Tree-Sitter** (`vtcode-core/src/tree_sitter/`, `vtcode-indexer/`): Rust, Python, JS/TS, Go, Java, Bash. Incremental AST with caching.\n\n**Code Intelligence** (`vtcode-core/src/tools/code_intelligence.rs`): `goto_definition`, `find_references`, `hover`, `document_symbol`, `workspace_symbol`.\n\n### Protocols\n\n**ACP** (`vtcode-acp-client/`): Zed IDE integration.\n\n**A2A**: Agent Card at `/.well-known/agent-card.json`. Task states, SSE, JSON-RPC 2.0. See `docs/a2a/a2a-protocol.md`.\n\n**MCP** (`vtcode-core/src/mcp/`): Extensible tooling via `rmcp`. Config: `.mcp.json` (project) + `vtcode.toml`. Transports: stdio, HTTP, child-process. See `docs/mcp/`.\n\n### Extensions\n\n**Skills** (`.vtcode/skills/` → `~/.vtcode/skills/` → embedded): agentskills.io standard. See `docs/skills/`.\n\n**Process Hardening** (`vtcode-process-hardening/`): Pre-main security (ptrace disable, core dump disable, env var removal). Exit codes: 5/6/7. See `docs/development/PROCESS_HARDENING.md`.\n\n## Code Style & Conventions\n\n### Code Style\n\n**Errors**: `anyhow::Result<T>` with `.with_context()`. Never `unwrap()`.\n\n**Unsafe Code**: Do not use `unsafe` blocks or `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` (including tests). Prefer safe std/crate APIs.\n\n**Config**: Never hardcode. Use `vtcode_core::config::constants`, `vtcode.toml`, or `docs/models.json`.\n\n**Naming**: `snake_case` functions/vars, `PascalCase` types. Descriptive names, early returns, 4-space indent.\n\n**Docs**: `.md` in `./docs/` only (`README.md` exception).\n\n### Rust Style (epage)\n\nBased on [epage's Rust Style Guide](https://epage.github.io/dev/rust-style/). Code is technical writing — lead with salient details (inverted pyramid), guide the reader through structure.\n\n#### Project Structure\n\n- **P-MOD**: Prefer `mod.rs` over `name.rs` when splitting into directories. Keeps modules atomic for browsing/renaming. Enforced via `clippy.self_named_module_files`.\n- **P-DIR-MOD**: Directory root modules (`mod.rs`, `lib.rs`) only re-export. All definitions live in topically named files.\n- **P-PRELUDE-MOD**: Prelude modules only re-export.\n- **P-PATH-MOD**: Avoid `#[path]`; use standard module lookup.\n- **P-API**: API should be a subset of file layout — a reader should navigate from API to source by path.\n- **P-VISIBILITY**: Limit visibility to module-scope (no `pub(_)`), `pub(crate)`, or `pub`. If not fully abstracted within a module, use `pub(crate)`.\n\n#### File Structure\n\n- **M-PRIV-PUB-USE**: Private imports first, then public re-exports (group `pub use` with the public API).\n- **M-PRIV-USE**: Limit private imports to heavily-used items where intent is clear from the name. Import traits anonymously (`use Trait as _`).\n- **M-SINGLE-USE**: Import items individually, not compound (`use a::B; use a::C;` not `use a::{B, C};`).\n- **M-ITEM-TOC**: Central/titular item first in a module — it provides context and a TOC for the rest.\n- **M-TYPE-ASSOC**: Type definition immediately followed by its `impl` block, before the next type.\n- **M-ASSOC-TRAIT**: Associated functions (`impl Foo {}`) before trait impls (`impl Display for Foo {}`).\n- **M-CALLER-CALLEE**: Caller before callee. The weaker the callee's abstraction, the closer it should follow.\n- **M-PUB-PRIV**: Public items before private items in modules, structs, and impl blocks.\n\n#### Function Structure\n\n- **F-GROUP**: Use blank lines to group related logic (\"paragraphs\" of a function).\n- **F-OUT**: Open blocks with output variable declarations to announce intent.\n- **F-VISUAL**: Blocks (`if`/`else`/`match`) should reflect business logic. Use early returns for bookkeeping, not business paths. Prefer combinators for non-business transformations.\n- **F-PURE-MUT**: Pure expressions xor mutable statements — don't mix mutation with expression-based logic.\n- **F-COMBINATOR**: Closures in combinators (`map`, `filter`, etc.) must be pure. Use `for` loops for side effects — `for_each`/`try_for_each` are disallowed via `clippy.toml`.\n\n### Logging & Tracing Guidelines\n\n**Architecture**: Shared buffered writer in `vtcode-core/src/utils/trace_writer.rs` (`FlushableWriter`) with flush hook in `vtcode-commons/src/trace_flush.rs`. Tracing setup is centralized in `src/main_helpers/tracing.rs` via `install_tracing_stack()`.\n\n**Level discipline** — choose the right level for each log:\n- `error!` — Unrecoverable failures that need operator attention\n- `warn!` — Degraded behavior (rate limits, circuit breakers, loop detection, config issues)\n- `info!` — Key lifecycle milestones (session start/end, approval decisions, feature activation)\n- `debug!` — Useful for troubleshooting (provider registration, cache hits, config loading)\n- `trace!` — Per-invocation internals (tool routing, policy checks, safety gateway, CGP lifecycle, payload diagnostics)\n\n**Rules**:\n- Never log at `info!` for per-tool-invocation events — use `trace!`\n- Never log at `debug!` for routine happy-path checks (read-only classification, preapproval) — use `trace!`\n- Keep `info!` for events that appear ≤ once per session phase (init, shutdown, mode change)\n- Actionable logs only: if the reader can't act on it, downgrade or remove it\n- No-op paths should be silent: early-return without logging when there's nothing to do (e.g., empty custom providers list)\n\n**Flush on exit**: Always call `vtcode_commons::trace_flush::flush_trace_log()` (or `vtcode_core::utils::trace_writer::flush_trace_log()`) before `process::exit()` or at shutdown. Signal handlers and TUI runner already do this.\n\n**Session file retention**: All session artifacts (`.json`, `.jsonl`, `.log`) are pruned by `session_archive` retention (default: 100 files, 14 days, 100 MB). Debug logs have separate rotation at 50 MB / 7 days.\n\n## Testing\n\n**Organization**: Unit tests inline (`#[cfg(test)]`), integration in `tests/`, benchmarks in `benches/`, snapshots via [`insta`](https://insta.rs).\n\n```bash\n# Build & check\ncargo build\ncargo check\n\n# Test (nextest is 3-5x faster)\ncargo nextest run              # All tests\ncargo nextest run -p vtcode-core  # Single package\ncargo nextest run --test integration_tests\ncargo nextest run -- --nocapture\n\n# Quick profiles (aliases)\ncargo t   # nextest run\ncargo tq  # no retries\ncargo ts  # fallback\n\n# Snapshots (insta)\ncargo insta test    # Run + review\ncargo insta review  # Interactive\ncargo insta accept  # Accept all\n\n# Benchmarks\ncargo bench\n\n# Quality gate\ncargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check && cargo check && cargo nextest run\n\n# Memory Safety (Miri)\n./scripts/check.sh miri  # Run local Miri check to detect Undefined Behavior\n```\n\n## Development Notes\n\n**Security**: Validate paths (workspace boundaries), command allowlists, tool policies in `vtcode.toml`, human-in-the-loop approval.\n\n**Performance**: Single codegen unit, strict Clippy, no `expect_used`/`unwrap_used`.\n\n### Performance & Simplicity\n\n- Don't speculate about bottlenecks; measure with VT Code benchmarks, traces, or targeted timings before optimizing.\n- Benchmark before tuning and keep before/after evidence for non-trivial performance claims.\n- Prefer simple or brute-force approaches when scale is unproven or inputs are usually small.\n- Avoid fancy algorithms unless measurements show they matter more than the simpler alternative.\n- Choose data structures and layout first; once the data is right, the algorithm should usually become obvious.\n\n**Blocked Handoff Recovery**: When a run writes a blocked handoff, check `.vtcode/tasks/current_blocked.md` (and `.vtcode/tasks/blockers/`) for context, then resume with `vtcode --resume <session_id>`.\n\n**Hooks Noise Control**: Set `hooks.lifecycle.quiet_success_output = true` in `vtcode.toml` to suppress plain stdout for successful lifecycle hooks while retaining structured/failed output.\n\n**Interactive Session Commands**: See `docs/user-guide/interactive-mode.md` for full details.\n\n- `/terminal-setup` runs the guided terminal setup flow for multiline bindings (see `docs/guides/terminal-optimization.md`).\n- `/vim`, `/vim on`, `/vim off` toggle Vim prompt editing; set `ui.vim_mode = true` in `vtcode.toml` to enable by default.\n- `/suggest`, `/tasks`, `/jobs` open prompt suggestions, the TODO panel, and the jobs picker.\n\n**Update System**: See `docs/guides/UPDATE_SYSTEM.md` for the full workflow.\n\n- `vtcode update` installs updates; `vtcode update --check` checks only.\n- `vtcode update --list` lists available versions; `vtcode update --list --limit <N>` lists more.\n- `vtcode update --pin <version>` pins a release; `vtcode update --unpin` clears the pin.\n- `vtcode update --channel <stable|beta|nightly>` switches release channels.\n\n## Agent-First CLI Invariants\n\n- Prefer machine-readable output for automation (`vtcode ask --output-format json`, `vtcode exec --json`).\n- Introspect tool schemas at runtime before generating tool calls (`vtcode schema tools`).\n- Use `vtcode exec --dry-run` before mutation-oriented autonomous tasks.\n- Assume agent-generated input can be adversarial; keep input validation strict for CLI/MCP fields.\n\n**Pitfalls**:\n\n1. Don't assume paths — validate boundaries\n2. Don't skip quality gate\n3. Don't assume `RwLock` is faster — benchmark; `Mutex` often wins\n\n### Slash Commands & Inline List UI\n\n- Register new slash command metadata in `vtcode-core/src/ui/slash.rs` (`SLASH_COMMANDS`) so it appears in the `/` suggestion list.\n- Wire command parsing and outcome routing end-to-end: `src/agent/runloop/slash_commands.rs` -> `src/agent/runloop/unified/turn/session/slash_commands/mod.rs` -> concrete handler in `.../slash_commands/ui.rs` (or relevant handler module).\n- For picker/selection UX, use shared inline list flows (`ShowListModal` / `ShowWizardModal` / shared inline events) instead of introducing new popup/overlay widget implementations.\n- Keep slash behavior consistent: if a command should execute immediately from slash selection, update the immediate-submit matcher in `vtcode-tui/src/core_tui/session/slash.rs`.\n- Add focused tests when touching this path (at minimum: `vtcode-core` slash suggestion tests and `vtcode-tui` slash/session tests).\n\n## Agent Execution Guidelines\n\n## Summary Instructions\n\nWhen VT Code compacts or summarizes a conversation, preserve:\n\n- The current task objective and acceptance criteria\n- File paths that were read or modified\n- Test results and error messages\n- Decisions made and the reasoning behind them\n\n### Task Execution\n\n- **Complete autonomously**: Resolve fully before yielding; no intermediate confirmations\n- **Root cause fixes**: Fix at the source, not surface-level\n- **Verify yourself**: Run `cargo check`, `cargo nextest`, `cargo clippy` after changes.\n- **No scope creep**: Stick to the task; don't fix unrelated issues (mention in final message).\n- **Testing**: Prefer cargo nextest for speed. Add tests if none exist, but don't add if it requires significant scope increase. Also run cargo insta snapshots if applicable for UI tests.\n- **Safety gate**: No `unsafe` code (including tests) and enforce `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`\n- **Precision over ambition**: Surgical changes respecting existing style\n- **Stay in scope**: Don't fix unrelated issues (mention in final message)\n- **Iterate proactively**: Fix errors without asking; know when \"done\" is sufficient\n\n### Responsiveness\n\n**Preambles** (before tool calls): 1-2 sentences showing progress. Group related actions. Skip for trivial reads.\n\n> \"Explored repo structure; checking LLM provider factory.\"\n> \"Config loads. Patching tool registry and tests.\"\n\n**Progress updates** for longer tasks: \"Finished tool trait analysis; implementing code_intelligence op.\"\n\n### Final Answers\n\n**Format**: Lead with outcomes. ≤10 lines. Use bullets (4-6 per section). Monospace for commands/paths.\n\n**Don't**: No inline citations, no repeating plans, no nested bullets, no cramming unrelated items.\n\n### Planning\n\nUse `update_plan` for 4+ step tasks. 5-7 word steps. Mark `in_progress`/`completed`. Update if scope changes.\n\n### Tool Use\n\n**Search**: `unified_search` with `action=\"grep\"` | `list` | `intelligence` | `tools` | `errors` | `agent`. Read files once.\n\n**Edit**: `unified_file` with `edit` | `write` | `create`. `unified_exec` for shell/PTY. Don't re-read to verify.\n\n**Test**: Specific → broad. Run proactively. No tests in codebase? Don't add. Run `cargo clippy` after changes.\n\n## Self-Documentation\n\nAnswering questions about VT Code? Check `docs/modules/vtcode_docs_map.md` first.\n\n## Model Management\n\nAdding a new LLM model requires updates across three layers (constants, configuration, runtime):\n\n- **Quick start**: `./scripts/add_model.sh` - Interactive prompts + code generation\n- **Full guide**: `docs/development/ADDING_MODELS.md` - 10-step detailed workflow\n- **Checklist**: `docs/development/MODEL_ADDITION_CHECKLIST.md` - 70-point verification\n- **Why partial automation**: `docs/development/MODEL_ADDITION_WORKFLOW.md` - Architecture & approach\n\nFiles to update: openai.rs, models.json, model_id.rs, as_str.rs, display.rs, description.rs, parse.rs, provider.rs, collection.rs, capabilities.rs.\n\n## Resources\n\n- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — High-level architecture\n- `docs/security/SECURITY_MODEL.md` — Security design\n- `docs/config/CONFIGURATION_PRECEDENCE.md` — Config loading order\n- `docs/providers/PROVIDER_GUIDES.md` — LLM provider setup\n- `docs/development/testing.md` — Testing strategy\n- `docs/development/ADDING_MODELS.md` — Model addition workflow\n\n## Hickey's Core: Simple > Easy\n\n**Simple** = one concern, not complected. **Easy** = familiar/convenient. Never conflate.\n\n**Rules**: Separate what changes from what doesn't. Data over objects. Values over mutation. No temporal coupling. Decomplect first.\n\n**Test**: Can you reason about this without loading the whole system? If no — it's complected.","status":"completed"}}}

When users ask questions about VT Code itself (capabilities, features, configuration, etc.), this file provides the complete catalog of available documentation sources.\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n**Core Questions**: Can VT Code do X? | How does VT Code Y work? | What's VT Code's Z feature?\n\n**Documentation Retrieval**: When users ask about VT Code capabilities, fetch relevant sections from the files listed below based on the topic area.\n\n## Documentation Categories\n\n### Configuration & Customization\n\n- **File**: `docs/config/CONFIG_FIELD_REFERENCE.md`\n  - **Content**: Config Field Reference\n  - **User Questions**: \"What can you tell me about Config Field Reference?\"\n\n- **File**: `docs/config/CONFIGURATION_PRECEDENCE.md`\n  - **Content**: Configuration Precedence in VT Code\n  - **Topics**: Resolution Order, Default Values, Validation, Environment Variables, Lifecycle Hooks Configuration\n  - **User Questions**: \"What can you tell me about Configuration Precedence in VT Code?\", \"How does Resolution Order work?\", \"How does Default Values work?\"\n\n- **File**: `docs/config/TOOLS_CONFIG.md`\n  - **Content**: Tools Configuration\n  - **User Questions**: \"What can you tell me about Tools Configuration?\"\n\n- **File**: `docs/config/config.md`\n  - **Content**: VT Code Configuration\n  - **Topics**: Quick navigation, Feature flags, Model selection, Execution environment, Context compaction and session history\n  - **User Questions**: \"What can you tell me about VT Code Configuration?\", \"How does Quick navigation work?\", \"How does Feature flags work?\"\n\n### Development & Testing\n\n- **File**: `docs/development/asset-synchronization.md`\n  - **Content**: **Asset Synchronization**\n  - **Topics**: **Overview**, **Why Asset Synchronization?**, **Synchronized Assets**, **Using the Sync Script**, **Automated Integration**\n  - **User Questions**: \"What can you tell me about **Asset Synchronization**?\", \"How does **Overview** work?\", \"How does **Why Asset Synchronization?** work?\"\n\n- **File**: `docs/development/testing.md`\n  - you tell me about Interactive Mode Reference?\", \"How does Keyboard Shortcuts work?\", \"How does Vim Mode work?\"\n\n## Enhanced Trigger Questions\n\n### Core Capabilities & Features\n- \"What can VT Code do?\"\n- \"What are VT Code's main features?\"\n- \"How does VT Code compare to other AI coding tools?\"\n- \"What makes VT Code unique?\"\n- \"Can VT Code handle multiple programming languages?\"\n- \"Does VT Code support real-time collaboration?\"\n- \"How does VT Code handle large codebases efficiently?\"\n- \"What are the different system prompt modes (minimal, lightweight, etc.)?\"\n- \"How can I reduce token usage with tool documentation modes?\"\n\n### Workflows & Agent Behavior\n- \"What is Plan Mode and how do I use it?\"\n- \"How do I use the @ symbol to reference files in my messages?\"\n- \"How do I use the /files slash command to browse my workspace?\"\n- \"What is the Decision Ledger and how does it help with coherence?\"\n- \"How does the agent handle long-running conversations?\"\n\n### Security & Reliability\n- \"What security layers does VT Code implement?\"\n- \"How does VT Code ensure shell command safety?\"\n- \"What is the 5-layer security model in VT Code?\"\n- \"How do tool policies and human-in-the-loop approvals work?\"\n- \"How does the circuit breaker prevent cascading failures?\"\n- \"Is my code and data safe with VT Code?\"\n\n### Integrations & Protocols\n- \"How do I use VT Code inside the Zed editor?\"\n- \"What is the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) and how is it used?\"\n- \"What is the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol?\"\n- \"How does VT Code conform to the Open Responses specification?\"\n- \"How do I configure Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers?\"\n- \"What are lifecycle hooks and how do I configure them?\"\n\n### Local Models & Providers\n- \"Can I use VT Code with local models via Ollama?\"\n- \"How do I integrate VT Code with LM Studio?\"\n- \"Which AI providers are supported (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.)?\"\n- \"How do I set up OpenRouter with VT Code?\"\n- \"How can I use Hugging Face Inference Providers?\"\n\n### Getting Started & Setup\n- \"How do I install VT Code?\"\n- \"How do I get started with VT Code?\"\n- \"How do I set up VT Code for the first time?\"\n- \"What do I need to get started?\"\n- \"How do I configure API keys?\"\n- \"Which LLM provider should I choose?\"\n- \"How do I configure VT Code for my workflow?\"\n- \"What are the most common keyboard shortcuts?\"\n\n### Development & Maintenance\n- \"How do I build VT Code from source?\"\n- \"How do I run the test suite?\"\n- \"How do I add a new tool to VT Code?\"\n- \"How do I debug agent behavior or tool execution?\"\n- \"How do I run the performance benchmarks?\"\n- \"How do I update the self-documentation map?\"\n- \"How do I contribute to the VT Code project?\"\n- \"What is the release process for VT Code?\"\n- \"How do I manage multi-crate dependencies in this workspace?\"\n\n## VT Code Feature Categories\n\n### Core Capabilities\n- **Multi-LLM Provider Support**: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, xAI, OpenRouter, Moonshot AI, Ollama, LM Studio\n- **Terminal Interface**: Modern TUI with mouse support, text selection, and streaming output\n- **Workspace Management**: Automatic project indexing, fuzzy file discovery, and context curation\n- **Tool System**: Modular, extensible tool architecture with 53+ specialized tools\n- **Security**: Enterprise-grade safety with tree-sitter-bash validation, sandboxing, and policy controls\n- **Agent Protocols**: Support for ACP, A2A, and Open Responses for cross-tool interoperability\n\n## Additional Resources\n\n### External Documentation\n- **Repository**: https://github.com/vinhnx/vtcode\n- **Crate**: https://crates.io/crates/vtcode\n- **VS Code Extension**: Open VSX and VS Code Marketplace\n\n---\n\n**Note**: This enhanced documentation map is designed for VT Code's self-documentation system. When users ask questions about VT Code itself, the system should fetch this document and use it to provide accurate, up-to-date information about VT Code's capabilities and features.","status":"completed"}}}

{"schema_version":"0.4.0","event":{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"call_4rtJmjWIeTyLCgQr8sGlr8nT:76fb2055","type":"tool_invocation","tool_name":"unified_file","arguments":{"action":"read","path":"docs/guides/agent-loop-contract.md","content":"","old_str":"","new_str":"","patch":"","destination":"","start_line":1,"end_line":200,"offset":0,"limit":0,"mode":"","condense":false,"indentation":false},"tool_call_id":"call_4rtJmjWIeTyLCgQr8sGlr8nT","status":"completed"}}}
{"schema_version":"0.4.0","event":{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"call_4rtJmjWIeTyLCgQr8sGlr8nT:76fb2055:output","type":"tool_output","call_id":"call_4rtJmjWIeTyLCgQr8sGlr8nT:76fb2055","tool_call_id":"call_4rtJmjWIeTyLCgQr8sGlr8nT","output":"# Agent Loop Contract\n\nVT Code keeps its existing harness-first runtime, but its external loop contract\nnow lines up more closely with SDK-style agent runtimes.\n\nThis guide describes the public lifecycle semantics shared by interactive runs,\n`vtcode exec`, harness logs, and Open Responses extension events.\n\n## Message and Event Mapping\n\nVT Code does not expose Claude-specific SDK structs. The canonical stream stays\n`vtcode_exec_events::ThreadEvent`.\n\nThe closest concept mapping is:\n\n| Agent SDK concept | VT Code event |\n| --- | --- |\n| `SystemMessage(init)` | `thread.started` |\n| `AssistantMessage` | `item.*` with `agent_message`, `reasoning`, `tool_invocation` |\n| Tool-result `UserMessage` | `item.*` with `tool_output` or `command_execution` |\n| `StreamEvent` | `item.updated` plus Open Responses stream events |\n| `ResultMessage` | `thread.completed` |\n| `compact_boundary` | `thread.compact_boundary` |\n\n`turn.started`, `turn.completed`, and `turn.failed` remain VT Code turn\nwrappers around the inner item lifecycle.\n\n## Terminal Thread Result\n\nVT Code now emits `thread.completed` at the end of a session or exec run.\n\nFields:\n\n- `thread_id`: stable event-stream thread identifier\n- `session_id`: stable VT Code session identifier\n- `subtype`: `success`, `error_max_turns`, `error_max_budget_usd`, `error_during_execution`, or `cancelled`\n- `outcome_code`: VT Code-specific terminal code\n- `result`: final assistant summary text on successful completion only\n- `stop_reason`: provider stop reason when available\n- `usage`: aggregate token usage for the full thread\n- `total_cost_usd`: aggregate estimated cost when pricing metadata exists\n- `num_turns`: total turn count\n\nFor `vtcode exec`, `outcome_code` comes from `TaskOutcome::code()`. Interactive\nsessions preserve the corresponding VT Code session end semantics.\n\n## Compaction Boundary\n\nWhenever VT Code compacts history itself or via a provider-native compaction\npath, it emits `thread.compact_boundary`.\n\nFields:\n\n- `thread_id`\n- `trigger`: `manual` or `auto`\n- `mode`: `local` or `provider`\n- `original_message_count`\n- `compacted_message_count`\n- `history_artifact_path`: optional archived history path\n\nThis is emitted for manual `/compact` flows and for automatic local fallback\ncompaction. When Open Responses is enabled, VT Code surfaces these as VT Code\ncustom extension events without changing the core Open Responses response model.\n\n## Budget and Limits\n\n`agent.harness.max_budget_usd` is the shared budget setting for interactive and\nexec sessions.\n\n- VT Code estimates cost from aggregate usage via `ModelResolver::estimate_cost`.\n- If pricing metadata is unavailable for the active model, VT Code does not\n  enforce the budget.\n- In that case `total_cost_usd` stays `null` and VT Code emits one warning.\n\nTurn limits still surface through `thread.completed.subtype = \"error_max_turns\"`.\n\n## Hooks\n\nVT Code now supports `hooks.lifecycle.pre_compact`.\n\n`pre_compact` runs before VT Code records a compaction boundary. Its payload\nincludes:\n\n- `session_id`\n- `cwd`\n- `hook_event_name = \"PreCompact\"`\n- `trigger`\n- `mode`\n- `original_message_count`\n- `compacted_message_count`\n- `history_artifact_path`\n- `transcript_path`\n\n`session_start` with source `compact` remains supported for compatibility, but\n`pre_compact` is the first-class hook for compaction-aware automation.\n\n## Related Controls\n\nThese VT Code settings line up with common agent-loop controls:\n\n- Tool allow and deny rules: `allowed_tools`, `disallowed_tools`, tool policy config\n- Permission mode: workspace trust, human-in-the-loop settings, full-auto allowlists\n- Effort: provider/model reasoning settings\n- Tool discovery: MCP and tool catalog flows\n- Resume and fork continuity: session archives, thread bootstrap, and compaction envelopes\n\nSubagents are unchanged in this pass.","status":"completed"}}}

{"schema_version":"0.4.0","event":{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"call_VURBAT3HBzuXMjy4gcy0wdsF:94367261:output","type":"tool_output","call_id":"call_VURBAT3HBzuXMjy4gcy0wdsF:94367261","tool_call_id":"call_VURBAT3HBzuXMjy4gcy0wdsF","output":"# VT Code Architecture Guide\n\n## Overview\n\nVT Code follows a modular architecture designed for maintainability,\nextensibility, and performance. Internally, VT Code uses Rust traits for\nruntime composition. Externally, VT Code prefers protocol- and manifest-driven\nextension seams such as MCP, skills, plugin manifests, and\n`[[custom_providers]]` so third-party integrations do not need to land new Rust\nimpls in the core workspace.\n\n## Model + Harness\n\nVT Code treats the model as the reasoning engine and the harness as the runtime that makes that reasoning useful. Phase 1 of the\nexec/full-auto runtime is built around that split rather than around a separate agent subsystem.\n\nHarness primitives in VT Code map to the runtime like this:\n\n- **Instruction memory**: AGENTS.md loading, project docs, prompt assembly, onboarding guidance, and session bootstrap live in `vtcode-core/src/prompts/`, `vtcode-core/src/core/agent/`, and the workspace instruction loaders.\n- **Tools**: `vtcode-tools/`, `vtcode-core/src/tools/`, MCP integration, slash commands, and the tool registry expose filesystem, search, edit, exec, and protocol-backed capabilities to the model.\n- **Sandbox / execution environment**: `vtcode-bash-runner/`, unified exec, workspace trust, command policies, and tool allow-lists define where generated code runs and what it can touch.\n- **Dynamic context**: context assembly, instruction merging, task tracker state, history, plan sidecars, and spooled tool outputs let VT Code rehydrate long-running work without keeping every token in the live window.\n- **Compaction / offloading**: split tool results, spool files, archive transcripts, and provider-aware auto-compaction reduce context rot while preserving recoverable state on disk.\n- **Hooks / middleware**: lifecycle hooks, tool middleware, guard rails, duplicate-call protection, and plan-mode enforcement add deterministic control around the model loop.\n- **Continuation**: exec/full-auto now uses a harness-managed continuation controller that accepts completion only when tracker state is complete and verification commands pass.\n- **Traces / archives**: thread events, session archives, checkpoints, Open Responses emission, and optional harness event logs capture what happened for resume, audit, and downstream tooling.\n\n`vtcode-exec-events::ThreadEvent` is the authoritative runtime event contract across exec mode, harness logs, and interactive lifecycle emission. Item lifecycle events come from the shared runtime/event builders, while outer `TurnStarted` / `TurnCompleted` / `TurnFailed` events remain wrapper-owned submission boundaries. Follow-up inputs are queued in the runtime and injected one-at-a-time only after a turn reaches an idle boundary.\n\nFor the public loop semantics and the Agent SDK concept mapping, see [Agent Loop Contract](./guides/agent-loop-contract.md).\n\nThe harness configuration is intentionally split across three existing surfaces instead of a new top-level subsystem:\n\n- `agent.harness` controls continuation, event logging, and per-turn safety limits.\n- `automation.full_auto` controls autonomous execution and turn limits.\n- `context.dynamic` controls how much state and history are reintroduced on each turn.\n\nThat split keeps VT Code aligned with the current runtime while making the harness explicit enough to evolve. Multi-agent\norchestration, dynamic tool assembly, and harness self-analysis remain follow-on work after single-agent long-horizon execution is\nstable.\n\n### Rig Alignment Boundary\n\nVT Code borrows selected request-shaping patterns from the Rig ecosystem without delegating runtime control to `rig-core` agents.\n\n- Rig-backed usage in VT Code is limited to provider/model validation and provider-specific reasoning payload shaping.\n- The VT Code harness remains the execution engine for turns, tool routing, continuation, compaction, and resume flows.\n- Session continuity stays on VT Code primitives: `.vtcode/history/` artifacts, local/server compaction, and prompt-side memory injection.\n- This keeps VT Code’s existing tool registry, sandboxing, and resume semantics intact while still adopting Rig’s provider-facing patterns where they reduce duplication.\n\n### CLI Architecture\n\nThe command-line interface is built on specific principles for robustness and interoperability:\n\n1.  **Strict Output Separation**: Data goes to `stdout`, diagnostics/logs go to `stderr`. This enables clean piping of machine-readable output.\n2.  **Standard Argument Parsing**: Uses `clap` for POSIX/GNU compliance, supporting standard flags and behavior.\n3.  **Command Isolation**: Each sub-command (`ask`, `exec`, `chat`) is handled by a dedicated module in `src/cli/`, sharing core logic via `vtcode-core`.\n4.  **Signal Handling**: Graceful handling of `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` to ensure resource cleanup (e.g., restoring terminal state).\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### TUI Architecture\n\nThe terminal UI now has a dedicated crate boundary:\n\n- `vtcode-tui`: public TUI-facing API surface for downstream consumers\n- `vtcode-core::ui::tui`: canonical runtime type surface for VT Code internals\n\nThis separation allows external code to import TUI types and session APIs from\n`vtcode-tui` while keeping host-specific integrations inside `vtcode-core`.\n\n`vtcode-tui` now exposes standalone launch primitives (`SessionOptions`,\n`SessionSurface`, `KeyboardProtocolSettings`) plus host adapters\n(`host::HostAdapter`) so downstream projects can start sessions without\nimporting `vtcode_core::config` types directly.\n\nThe full TUI source tree is now located in:\n\n- `vtcode-tui/src/core_tui/`\n\n`vtcode-core/src/ui/tui.rs` is a compatibility shim that compiles this migrated\nsource tree to preserve existing `vtcode_core::ui::tui` paths.\n\nThe TUI runner is organized into focused modules:\n\n```\nvtcode-tui/src/core_tui/runner/\n mod.rs           # Orchestration entrypoint (`run_tui`)\n drive.rs         # Main terminal/event loop drive logic\n events.rs        # Async event stream + tick scheduling\n signal.rs        # SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup guard\n surface.rs       # Inline vs alternate screen detection\n terminal_io.rs   # Cursor/screen prep + drain helpers\n terminal_modes.rs# Raw/mouse/focus/keyboard mode management\n```\n\n### Modular Tools System\n\n```\ntools/\n mod.rs           # Module coordination & exports\n traits.rs        # Core composability traits\n types.rs         # Common types & structures\n cache.rs         # Enhanced caching system\n grep_file.rs     # Ripgrep-backed search manager\n file_ops.rs      # File operations tool (async + cached)\n command.rs       # Command execution tool (3 modes)\n registry.rs      # Tool coordination & function declarations\n```\n\n### Core Traits\n\n```rust\n// Base tool interface\npub trait Tool: Send + Sync {\n    async fn execute(&self, args: Value) -> Result<Value>;\n    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n    fn description(&self) -> &'static str;\n}\n\n// Multi-mode execution\npub trait ModeTool: Tool {\n    fn supported_modes(&self) -> Vec<&'static str>;\n    async fn execute_mode(&self, mode: &str, args: Value) -> Result<Value>;\n}\n\n// Intelligent caching\npub trait CacheableTool: Tool {\n    fn cache_key(&self, args: &Value) -> String;\n    fn should_cache(&self, args: &Value) -> bool;\n}\n```\n\n## Tool Implementations\n\n### GrepSearchManager (`tools::grep_file`)\n\n-   Debounce and cancellation pipeline for responsive searches\n-   Ripgrep primary backend with perg fallback when unavailable\n-   Supports glob filters, hidden file handling, and context lines\n-   Enforces workspace boundaries with robust path validation\n\n### Search Stack Consolidation\n\n-   `grep_file.rs` is the single source of truth for content search\n-   Higher-level helpers were removed; use `ToolRegistry::grep_file_executor`\n-   `list_files` remains a discovery/metadata tool; defer all content scanning to `grep_file`\n\n### FileOpsTool\n\n-   Workspace-scoped file listing, metadata inspection, and traversal\n-   Async directory walking with cache integration for large trees\n-   Path policy enforcement shared with the command subsystem\n\n### CommandTool\n\n-   Standard command execution with exit-code and output capture\n-   PTY session management for interactive commands\n-   Streaming support for long-lived shell tasks with cancellation\n\n## Design Principles\n\n1. **Internal Traits, External Protocols** - Keep Rust traits as internal composition seams; prefer config, manifests, and protocols for third-party extension points\n2. **Mode-based Execution** - Single tools support multiple execution modes\n3. **Simplicity First** - Prefer simple algorithms and control flow until real workload data justifies more complexity\n4. **Data-Oriented Design** - Choose data structures and boundaries so the right algorithm is obvious\n5. **Backward Compatibility** - All existing APIs remain functional\n6. **Measured Optimization** - Profile and benchmark before keeping performance-motivated complexity\n7. **Clear Separation** - Each module has single responsibility\n\n## Adding New Tools\n\n```rust\nuse super::traits::{Tool, ModeTool};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\n\npub struct MyTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl Tool for MyTool {\n    async fn execute(&self, args: Value) -> Result<Value> {\n        // Implementation\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &'static str { \"my_tool\" }\n    fn description(&self) -> &'static str { \"My custom tool\" }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ModeTool for MyTool {\n    fn supported_modes(&self) -> Vec<&'static str> {\n        vec![\"mode1\", \"mode2\"]\n    }\n\n    async fn execute_mode(&self, mode: &str, args: Value) -> Result<Value> {\n        match mode {\n            \"mode1\" => self.execute_mode1(args).await,\n            \"mode2\" => self.execute_mode2(args).await,\n            _ => Err(anyhow::anyhow!(\"Unsupported mode: {}\", mode))\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Benefits\n\n-   **77% complexity reduction** from monolithic structure\n-   **Enhanced functionality** through mode-based execution\n-   **100% backward compatibility** maintained\n-   **Protocol-friendly extension model** for external development\n-   **Performance optimized** with intelligent caching\n\n## RL Optimization Loop (Adaptive Action Selection)\n\n-   **Module:** `vtcode-core/src/llm/rl` (bandit and actor-critic implementations)\n-   **Config:** `[optimization]` with `strategy = \"bandit\" | \"actor_critic\"` plus reward shaping knobs\n-   **Signals:** Success/timeout + latency feed `RewardSignal`, stored in a rolling `RewardLedger`\n-   **Usage:** Construct `RlEngine::from_config(&VTCodeConfig::optimization)` and call `select(actions, PolicyContext)`; on completion emit `apply_reward`\n-   **Goal:** Prefer low-latency, high-success actions (e.g., choose edge vs cloud executors) while remaining pluggable for future policies\n\n## Training & Evaluation Alignment\n\nTo operationalize the staged training paradigm introduced in `docs/research/kimi_dev_agentless_training.md`, VT Code couples its\nmodular runtime with a data and evaluation strategy designed for agentless skill priors:\n\n-   **Dual Roles** – Prompt templates for `BugFixer` and `TestWriter` share the same tool registry, enabling deterministic skill\n    acquisition before agentic orchestration.\n-   **Execution Telemetry** – Command and sandbox outputs are captured through the existing `bash_runner` and PTY subsystems,\n    allowing outcome-based rewards for RL without extra instrumentation.\n-   **Self-Play Hooks** – The tool layer exposes high-signal search, diff, and patching capabilities that feed directly into the\n    multi-rollout evaluation loop defined in the training roadmap.\n-   **Context Capacity** – Long-context support in the LLM provider abstraction ensures that multi-turn reasoning traces and\n    aggregated rollouts remain accessible during both SFT and inference.\n\nSee the research note for the full pipeline (mid-training data curation, SFT cold start, RL curriculum, and test-time self-play).","status":"completed"}}}

    "  Core Agent Loop Summary",
    "",
    "  Based on the codebase exploration:",
    "",
    "  Architecture Overview",
    "",
    "  The core agent system is in vtcode-core/src/core/agent/:",
    "",
    "  • core.rs - Main Agent struct with builder pattern for initialization",
    "  • runner.rs - AgentRunner that orchestrates individual agent instances",
    "  • runtime/mod.rs - The runtime execution engine with the turn-based loop",
    "  ",
    "  Key Components",
    "",
    "  1. Turn-based execution: The loop processes turns via run_turn_once_with_adapter<A> which:",
    "",
    "    ◦ Takes an LLMRequest",
    "    ◦ Calls adapter.execute() to run the model",
    "    ◦ Tracks progress via record_model_progress()",
    "    ◦ Returns TurnExecution with response content",
    "    ",
    "  2. Control flow: poll_turn_control() manages pause/resume/stop via SteeringMessage",
    "",
    "  3. Lifecycle tracking: SharedLifecycleEmitter records reasoning stages, tool calls, and emits events",
    "",
    "  4. Event system: EventSink and events module handle tool call status (ToolCallStatus), thread events",
    "",
    "  5. Tool integration: ToolRegistry manages available tools (1407 CLI tools discovered)",
    "",

    "",

      "role": "Tool",
      "content": "{\"content\":\"//! # Core Agent Architecture\\n//!\\n//! This module contains the core components of the VT Code agent system,\\n//! implementing the main agent loop, context management, and supporting infrastructure.\\n//!\\n//! ## Architecture Overview\\n//!\\n//! The core system is built around several key components:\\n//!\\n//! - **Agent**: Main agent implementation with conversation management\\n\\n//! - **Prompt Caching**: Strategic caching for improved response times\\n//! - **Decision Tracking**: Audit trail of agent decisions and actions\\n//! - **Error Recovery**: Intelligent error handling with context preservation\\n//! - **Timeout Detection**: Prevents runaway operations\\n//! - **Trajectory Management**: Session state and history tracking\\n//!\\n//! ## Key Components\\n//!\\n//! ### Agent System\\n… [+34 lines omitted; use read_file with offset/limit (1-indexed line numbers) for full content]\\npub use context_optimizer::ContextOptimizer;\\npub use execution_context::ExecutionContext;\\npub use memory_pool::{MemoryPool, global_pool};\\npub use optimized_agent::{AgentContext, AgentState, OptimizedAgentEngine};\\npub use performance_profiler::{BenchmarkResults, BenchmarkUtils, PerformanceProfiler};\\npub use threads::{\\n    SubmissionId, ThreadBootstrap, ThreadEventRecord, ThreadId, ThreadManager, ThreadRuntimeHandle,\\n    ThreadSnapshot, build_thread_archive_metadata, loaded_skills_from_session_listing,\\n    messages_from_session_listing,\\n};\",\"path\":\"vtcode-core/src/core/mod.rs\",\"encoding\":\"utf8\",\"content_kind\":\"text\"}",
      "tool_call_id": "call_function_iu4sphpzuimb_2"

Agent system for intelligent conversation management\\n\\npub mod blocked_handoff;\\npub mod bootstrap;\\npub mod chat;\\npub mod completion;\\npub mod harness_artifacts;\\n\\npub mod config;\\npub mod conversation;\\npub mod core;\\npub mod display;\\npub mod error_recovery;\\npub mod events;\\npub mod features;\\npub mod runner;\\npub mod runtime;\\npub mod session;\\npub mod session_config;\\npub mod snapshots;\\npub mod state;\\npub mod stats;\\npub mod steering;\\npub mod task;\\npub mod types;\\n\\n// Re-export main types for convenience\\npub use bootstrap::{AgentComponentBuilder, AgentComponentSet};\\npub use features::{FeatureGate, FeatureSet, FeatureStage, OpenResponsesFeature};\\npub use session_config::ResolvedSessionConfig;\\n\\npub use types::*;\",\"path\":\"vtcode-core/src/core/agent/mod.rs\",\"encoding\":\"utf8\",\"content_kind\":\"text\"}",

      "content": "## Core Agent Loop Summary\n\nBased on the codebase exploration:\n\n### Architecture Overview\n\nThe core agent system is in `vtcode-core/src/core/agent/`:\n\n- **`core.rs`** - Main `Agent` struct with builder pattern for initialization\n- **`runner.rs`** - `AgentRunner` that orchestrates individual agent instances\n- **`runtime/mod.rs`** - The runtime execution engine with the turn-based loop\n\n### Key Components\n\n1. **Turn-based execution**: The loop processes turns via `run_turn_once_with_adapter<A>` which:\n   - Takes an `LLMRequest`\n   - Calls `adapter.execute()` to run the model\n   - Tracks progress via `record_model_progress()`\n   - Returns `TurnExecution` with response content\n\n2. **Control flow**: `poll_turn_control()` manages pause/resume/stop via `SteeringMessage`\n\n3. **Lifecycle tracking**: `SharedLifecycleEmitter` records reasoning stages, tool calls, and emits events\n\n4. **Event system**: `EventSink` and `events` module handle tool call status (`ToolCallStatus`), thread events\n\n5. **Tool integration**: `ToolRegistry` manages available tools (1407 CLI tools discovered)\n\n### Entry Point\n\n```\nsrc/cli/init.rs → crate::agent::agents::run_single_agent_loop()\n```\n\n### Supporting Systems\n\n- **Loop detector** (`loop_detector.rs`) - Prevents repetitive behavior\n- **Error recovery**",

This module contains the core components of the VT Code agent system,\n//! implementing the main agent loop, context management, and supporting infrastructure.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture Overview\n//!\n//!
